line: - Added session discovery/selection primitives and recency ordering in `src/cmd/common.rs:55`, `src/cmd/common.rs:163`, `src/cmd/common.rs:663`, `src/cmd/common.rs:722`, `src/cmd/common.rs:808`.
- `saw watch` now supports `--session <uuid>` and defaults to the most recent matching session in the cwd via `src/cmd/watch.rs:61` and `src/cmd/watch.rs:269`.

- `saw status` now supports `--session <uuid>` and `--all`; single-session status prompts when multiple matching sessions exist, while `--all` renders all matching sessions via `src/cmd/status.rs:24`, `src/cmd/status.rs:27`, `src/cmd/status.rs:62`.
- Explicit session snapshots now preserve live PID/process metrics even when multiple sessions share one cwd via `src/cmd/common.rs:163`.
- Used `(started_at, jsonl mtime)` as the recency key so default selection is stable and still prefers the most recently active session.

items: Used `(started_at, jsonl mtime)` as the recency key so default selection is stable and still prefers the most recently active session.

line: - Kept `watch` non-interactive by defaulting to the most recent session; applied the selection prompt to `status` for the “show selection prompt” acceptance behavior.

items: Kept `watch` non-interactive by defaulting to the most recent session; applied the selection prompt to `status` for the “show selection prompt” acceptance behavior.

line: - Added a shared HOME-env test lock to avoid flaky tests that mutate `HOME`.

items: Added a shared HOME-env test lock to avoid flaky tests that mutate `HOME`.

line: - Tests that mutate `HOME` were racing; serializing them was necessary for stable session/config tests.

items: Tests that mutate `HOME` were racing; serializing them was necessary for stable session/config tests.

line: - Recency based only on filesystem mtime was too flaky in tests; combining it with `startedAt` made ordering deterministic.

items: Recency based only on filesystem mtime was too flaky in tests; combining it with `startedAt` made ordering deterministic.

line: - The selection prompt behavior is implemented for `saw status` when multiple cwd-matching sessions exist. `saw watch` intentionally remains non-interactive and auto-selects the most recent session per the task requirement.

items: The selection prompt behavior is implemented for `saw status` when multiple cwd-matching sessions exist. `saw watch` intentionally remains non-interactive and auto-selects the most recent session per the task requirement.
